facts.statement: Caterpillar Inc. is listed on the NYSE under ticker CAT, and its SEC SIC description is "Construction Machinery & Equip."
Caterpillar reported FY2025 revenue of $67.060 billion, filed on 2026-02-13 under the Revenues concept.
Caterpillar reported FY2025 operating cash flow of $12.885 billion, filed on 2026-02-13 under NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities.
Caterpillar reported FY2025 research and development expense of $2.108 billion, filed on 2026-02-13.
At FY2025, Caterpillar reported $6.889 billion of cash and equivalents and $27.351 billion of long-term debt.

Caterpillar's 2025 Form 10-K XBRL tags identify operating segment categories including Construction Industries, Resource Industries, Power & Energy, All Other Segments, Machinery/Power/Energy, and Financial Products.
Caterpillar's 2025 Form 10-K includes Financial Products finance receivable categories for wholesale receivables, wholesale loans, wholesale leases, retail loans, and retail leases.

Caterpillar's 2025 Form 10-K XBRL tags include sales or finance receivable location categories for the United States, countries outside the United States, North America, EMEA, Asia Pacific, Latin America, China, Brazil, India, and Switzerland.

Caterpillar's 2026 proxy statement says 2025 sales and revenues were $67.6 billion, the highest full-year sales and revenues in company history, and backlog reached $51 billion, up 71% year over year.

Caterpillar's 2026 proxy statement reports 2025 operating profit of $11.2 billion, adjusted operating profit of $11.6 billion, operating profit margin of 16.5%, and adjusted operating profit margin of 17.2%.
Caterpillar's 2026 proxy statement reports 2025 profit per share of $18.81 versus $22.05 in 2024, and adjusted profit per share of $19.06 versus $21.90 in 2024.

facts.topic: Free cash flow and shareholder returns
facts.statement: Caterpillar's 2026 proxy statement reports 2025 MP&E free cash flow of $9.5 billion and deployment of $7.9 billion to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends.
Caterpillar's 2026 proxy statement reports an enterprise cash balance of $10.0 billion at the end of 2025.

Caterpillar reported restructuring costs of $448 million in 2025, $359 million in 2024, and $780 million in 2023; 2025 restructuring included companywide actions and inventory write-downs in the Rail division.
Caterpillar's 2025 Form 10-K says all restructuring costs in 2025, 2024, and 2023 were excluded from segment profit.

On February 3, 2026, the Federal Court of Australia approved Caterpillar's acquisition of RPMGlobal Holdings Limited, an Australian software company, with an expected purchase price of approximately $790 million excluding cash acquired.

Caterpillar's 2025 Form 10-K describes RPMGlobal as a leading provider of mining software solutions with domain expertise in mining technology enablement and data-driven software solutions at every stage of the mining lifecycle.
Joseph E. Creed is Caterpillar's Chairman and CEO in the 2026 proxy statement; the proxy says he became chairman of the board on April 1, 2026, succeeding Jim Umpleby.
The 2026 proxy statement says Jim Umpleby retired as Executive Chairman on April 1, 2026, after more than four decades with Caterpillar, including eight years as Chairman and CEO.

Caterpillar's 2025 Form 10-K was signed by Joseph E. Creed as Chief Executive Officer and Director, Andrew R.J. Bonfield as Chief Financial Officer, William E. Schaupp as Vice President and Chief Accounting Officer, Derek Owens as Chief Legal Officer and General Counsel, and D. James Umpleby III as Executive Chairman.

Caterpillar's 2026 proxy lists 10 director nominees, 9 independent directors, an average director age of 65, average nominee tenure of 5.5 years, 50% women nominees, and 30% ethnic/racial diversity among nominees.

Caterpillar's 2026 proxy governance highlights include annual director elections, majority voting in director elections, a lead independent director, proxy access, shareholder ability to call special meetings, no poison pill, director and executive officer stock ownership guidelines, anti-hedging and pledging policies, and a clawback policy.

As of December 31, 2025, Caterpillar had 4,710,403 securities to be issued upon exercise of outstanding options, warrants and rights under shareholder-approved equity compensation plans, a weighted-average exercise price of $220.52, and 39,336,515 securities remaining available for future issuance; no equity compensation plans not approved by shareholders were listed.

Caterpillar disclosed that it maintains trademarks in Iran through counsel and service providers in the United Arab Emirates and Iran, has an OFAC specific license for payments to Iran's Intellectual Property Center at Bank Melli, paid approximately $608 in the period ended December 31, 2024, and generates no revenues or profits from this activity.
